task: Write a Python function second_largest(nums) returning the second largest distinct value in the list, or None when there are fewer than two distinct values. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The provided function correctly handles edge cases, such as a list with fewer than two distinct values, and the code is mostly clear in its intent.
trace: To solve this task, I took advantage of the concept that when writing a function to find distinct values, each value should be counted only once. This was achieved by sorting the input list and iterating through it in reverse order while keeping track of the first and second largest unique values. An edge case was considered where there are fewer than two distinct values in the list, in which case we return None instead of a valid answer.
